title: Preparation of Binding Buffer (BB)
author: Nicola O'Reilly
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bfd2ji8e

steps.instruction: Please select between the recipes for 2 L + 80 ml Binding buffer BB, 4 L + 160 ml Binding buffer BB and 5 L + 200 ml Binding buffer BB.
Weigh out 955.2 g, GuHCl and add to a 2 L beaker.
Add 500 mL, freshly drawn milliQ . Stir and heat to 60 °C to dissolve.
Add 80 mL, 3M sodium acetate , pH 5.2 .
Add 1 mL, Tween-20 . Stir well to mix.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store Binding Buffer BB buffer in a flammables cupboard at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
Add 1000 mL, freshly drawn milliQ water to a 5 L beaker.
Weigh out 1.911 kg, GuHCl in aliquots using a 3 L beaker and carefully add to 5 L beaker. Stir and heat to 60 °C to dissolve. A sterile pipette may be required to stir.
Once dissolved, make up to 4 L with Isopropanol (approx. 1600 mL ) using a 1 L graduated cylinder and transferring buffer to second 5 L beaker.
Add 160 mL, 3M sodium acetate , pH 5.2 .
Add 2 mL, Tween-20 . Stir well to mix.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store Binding Buffer BB buffer in a flammables cupboard at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
Once dissolved, make up to 2 L with Isopropanol (approx. 800 mL ) using a 1 L graduated cylinder.
Add 1250 mL, freshly drawn milliQ water to a 5 L beaker.
Weigh out 2.388 kg, GuHCl in aliquots using a 3 L beaker and carefully add to 5 L beaker. Stir and heat to 60 °C to dissolve. A sterile pipette may be required to stir.
Once dissolved, make up to 5 L with Isopropanol (approx. 2 L ) using a 1 L graduated cylinder and transferring buffer to second 5 L beaker.
Add 200 mL, 3M sodium acetate , pH 5.2 .
Add 2.5 mL, Tween-20 . Stir well to mix.
Vacuum filter into sterile bottles.
Label with batch and date.
Store Binding Buffer BB buffer in a flammables cupboard at Room temperature ( 15 °C – 25 °C ).
